project_index.scope: paper modules M1..M12 only; NOT a general project-wide index
project_index.note: There is no general agent-acceptance-wide Project Index file; the only Project Index is paper-scoped. HANDOFF compiler will need to either build a synthetic project-wide view from .ai/tasks/ + ledgers + git log, or document that no general index exists.

candidates: .ai/module_ledger/ (M1.json, M2.json, M3.json) — paper module per-module ledger
.ai/tasks/ (24 task yaml files) — task spec / state per task
docs/WORKFLOW_AUDIT_LEDGER.yaml (mentioned in BOOT_CONTEXT step 5, not yet verified)
issue_ledger.authoritative_pick: unverified — needs Phase 1 inspection to choose canonical ledger

primary_storage_patterns: evidence_packs/GPT_REPLY_56.txt, GPT_REPLY_57.txt — numbered raw replies at evidence_packs root
GPT_*.txt at repo root (≥24 files) — older / ad-hoc captured replies, MOSTLY UNTRACKED

gpt_review_transaction_runner.pipeline: pre-gate → linter → (CDP submit — manual) → verify reply → closure check

reuse_decisions.delta_justification: Only need a NEW orchestrator that: (a) calls build_boot_context as the trunk, (b) injects source-of-truth priority section, (c) emits PASTE_BLOCK_DRAFT_FOR_NEW_GPT.txt and HANDOFF_EVIDENCE_MAP.json, (d) refuses to mark output 'approved'. Do not rewrite the compression or boot-context logic.
reuse_decisions.naming_collision: none — handoff_compiler.py does not exist yet

reuse_decisions.delta_justification: No existing script compares Project Index / verdict timestamps / test counts across BOOT_CONTEXT vs memory/index.md vs PROJECT_HISTORY.md. This is a real gap. CONCRETE EVIDENCE for the need: BOOT_CONTEXT.md itself contains BOTH '232 PASS' (line 29) AND '247 PASS' (line 35) — an in-file contradiction that current tooling does not detect.

existing_assets: scripts/validate_context_memory.py (FAIL_CLOSED_PATTERNS for paper text, transcripts, secrets)
reuse_decisions.delta_justification: validate_context_memory.py already implements exactly the required FAIL_CLOSED scan (real_paper_full_text, 博士论文正文, 用户论文全文, api_key, etc.). Recommended path: write a 30-50 line WRAPPER that calls validate_context_memory.check() against the handoff draft + paste block and emits HANDOFF_SAFETY_SCAN.md. Do NOT create a parallel pattern list — that risks drift between the two safety guards.

No existing script enumerates the P0/P1/P2/P3 source-of-truth hierarchy and maps draft handoff claims back to evidence. This is the core new artifact the task requires. Output: HANDOFF_EVIDENCE_MAP.json with claim → {source_layer, source_path, sha256, timestamp}.

boot_context.schema.json validates BOOT_CONTEXT structure; this new schema describes the handoff_compiler.py output envelope (draft_path, paste_block_path, stale_check_ref, safety_scan_ref, source_map_ref, approval_status='draft_only'). Recommend reusing field names from boot_context.schema.json where they overlap.
Distinct shape from review_result: maps each handoff claim → source layer/path/sha256. No analog exists.

First-of-its-kind: model capability observation log entry. Must enforce 8 dimensions (Evidence-First, Stale identification, Safety boundary, Task planning, Repair quality, Loop control, Command discipline, Honesty) and the required fields (observation_id, date, task_id, submitted_claim, evidence_received, GPT_verdict, strengths, weaknesses, error_patterns, reliability_score_1_to_5, notes_for_future_model_assessment).
Existing tests do NOT exercise the new orchestrators. Follow TDD per global rule gsd-tdd: RED → GREEN → REGRESSION. Suggest each test file 150-300 LoC with synthetic fixtures (NOT real paper text).

stale_findings.evidence: Line 29 states '当前 agent-acceptance 测试 232 PASS (170 baseline + 62 new)' AND line 35 states '247 tests PASS' — same file, two different totals.
stale_findings.implication: Any handoff generated from BOOT_CONTEXT inherits an internal contradiction. Stale check must surface this.

stale_findings.evidence: All three quote '247 PASS'. No script has been run during Phase 0 to re-collect the real current count. Memory compiler outputs were last updated 2026-06-07.
stale_findings.implication: Memory layer (P2) and BOOT_CONTEXT layer (P1) agree, but neither is re-verified against a fresh pytest run (P0). Standard stale-check practice: re-run pytest --co (collect-only) or last test manifest, compare.

stale_findings.evidence: Previous coding-agent reply (this session, before Phase 0) cited '296 PASS' as a fact. Phase 0 found NO written source supporting 296. The agent (minimax-m3) likely fabricated or hallucinated this number from conversational context, NOT from any P0/P1/P2 evidence.
stale_findings.implication: Strong evidence for why memory compiler / coding-agent summaries cannot be promoted to P0. This case will be the first entry in MINIMAX_M3_OBSERVATION_LOG once Phase 1 is authorized.

stale_findings.evidence: BOOT_CONTEXT says 'PROJECT-HISTORY-BOOT-MEMORY-SYNC-A1 已完成' and recommends waiting for next GPT-authorized task. But git log shows the last 10 commits are ALL paper module work (M1-M12 R2/R3 reviews) committed AFTER BOOT_CONTEXT's claimed date 2026-06-08. BOOT_CONTEXT is at least a few commits stale w.r.t. real work in progress.
stale_findings.implication: Stale check must compare BOOT_CONTEXT 'last updated' vs HEAD commit date.

stale_findings.evidence: paper_privacy.md states '论文处理被 freeze. 不进行 PAPER-C3 / 真实论文处理'. But evidence_packs/paper-c3-dry-run/ exists, _reports/PAPER_PROJECT_INDEX.json shows 12 active paper modules with M1-M12 in_progress status, and last 10 commits are paper reviews.
stale_findings.implication: Memory layer says 'frozen', repo state says 'active'. Stale check or freeze-status reconciliation needed.

conflicts_and_high_risk_operations.naming_conflicts: none — all proposed scripts/schemas/tests have unique names not present in repo.
conflicts_and_high_risk_operations.schema_conflicts: none — but RECOMMEND aligning field names with existing boot_context.schema.json and gpt_review_result.schema.json to reduce semantic drift.
conflicts_and_high_risk_operations.old_handoff_misuse_risk: HIGH — HANDOFF.md / HANDOFF_V5.md / HANDOFF_V6.md / PROJECT_HISTORY.md / PROJECT_HISTORY_FINAL.md all sit at repo root with no SUPERSEDED notice. A new agent reading the repo cold could pick any of them as 'current'. LEGACY_HANDOFF_INVENTORY.md (Phase 1+ deliverable) is needed.
human_required_decisions: Phase 1 must decide: do we use a paper-scoped Project Index (existing) or build a general project-wide index? Affects HANDOFF_EVIDENCE_MAP design.
Phase 1 must decide: which is the canonical issue ledger — .ai/module_ledger/, .ai/tasks/, tasks/, or docs/WORKFLOW_AUDIT_LEDGER.yaml? They appear to track different things.

Phase 1 must decide: does 'paper processing frozen' (per memory) override the recent paper M1-M12 commits, or vice versa? This is a project-state question, not a code question — user/GPT must clarify.

blockers: User must resolve the 'paper processing frozen vs M1-M12 active commits' contradiction (STALE-05) before HANDOFF_DRAFT can honestly describe current module status.
User must indicate canonical issue ledger choice (or accept agent's inference: prefer .ai/module_ledger/ for paper modules + .ai/tasks/ for governance tasks).

evidence_pack_last: evidence_packs/handoff-pipeline-refactor-a1/{actual_deliverables/, reports/, CLOSURE_REPORT.md, GPT_REVIEW_PROMPT.md, PACK_MANIFEST.md, SAFETY_ATTESTATION.md, closure-pack.zip}

explicitly_forbidden_in_phase1: delete/move/rename HANDOFF.md, HANDOFF_V5.md, HANDOFF_V6.md, PROJECT_HISTORY.md, PROJECT_HISTORY_FINAL.md, any GPT_*.txt at root
rewrite content inside legacy files — only append SUPERSEDED notice if user explicitly authorizes that sub-step
promote any draft to *_APPROVED_* before a captured GPT verdict exists
include real paper full text, original paragraphs, advisor comments in any new artifact
